meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa says he will leave role after poor season
items.summary: Arbeloa says he will not be coaching the team next season, amid reports that Jose Mourinho is returning to the club.

items.title: Tuchel leaves Palmer and Foden out of England’s 2026 World Cup squad
items.summary: Real Madrid's Trent Alexander-Arnold also left out, while Saudi-based Al-Ahli striker Ivan Toney the surprise inclusion.

items.title: US deepens European uncertainty with deployment of 5,000 troops to Poland
items.summary: Erratic US policy moves leave European NATO partners increasingly unclear about which areas they should prioritise.

items.title: Lebanon’s economy struggles under renewed war and global fuel crisis
items.summary: Lebanon's ongoing war and financial crises threaten to render the nation economically unviable, experts warn.

items.title: US raises threat of military action against Cuba
items.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says Cuba poses a national security threat.

items.title: Japan’s World Cup 2026 team preview: Players to watch, group, squad
items.summary: Japan have beaten Germany, Brazil, England and Spain since 2022, but may struggle to sustain a genuine title push.

items.title: Iran war day 84: US-Iran talks advance amid mediation push
items.summary: US and Iran continue mediated talks, exchanging draft proposals aimed at reaching a formal agreement.

items.title: Monfils warms up for final French Open with star-studded farewell party
items.summary: The evening blended tennis, ​music and tributes to celebrate his two-decade journey on the professional tour.

items.title: Two-time NASCAR champ Kyle Busch dies at 41 after ‘severe illness’
items.summary: Busch was in his 22nd full-time season in NASCAR's top division, where he won two Cup Series titles in 2015 and 2019.

items.title: Knicks move two wins from the NBA Finals with 109-93 victory over the Cavs
items.summary: New York Knicks use an explosive third quarter to take a 2-0 lead over Cleveland in the NBA Eastern Conference finals.

items.title: Thousands attend funeral prayers for San Diego mosque attack victims
items.summary: More than 2,000 people gathered for Islamic funeral prayers in San Diego for three men killed in mosque attack

items.title: Turkish opposition leader vows to stay after court ousts him
items.summary: Turkiye’s main opposition leader Ozgur Ozel has vowed not to leave party headquarters.

items.title: US pausing $14bn arms sale to Taiwan due to Iran war, navy chief says
items.summary: Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao's remarks come as US President Donald Trump gives mixed signals on the sale.

items.title: Malaysia orders TikTok to address ‘defamatory’ content about king
items.summary: Watchdog instructs social media giant to strengthen moderation following circulation of 'grossly offensive' content.

items.title: Caitriona Graham recounts violent treatment after Gaza flotilla raid
items.summary: Irish activist Caitriona Graham recounts violent treatment after Gaza flotilla raid

items.title: Greenlanders protest opening of new US consulate in Nuuk
items.summary: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Nuuk as the United States opened a new consulate in Greenland’s capital.

items.title: Turkish court ousts leader of main opposition party
items.summary: Ankara court overturns results of leadership election won by Republican People's Party head Ozgur Ozel.

items.title: US Senate pushes back against Trump’s $1.8bn ‘anti-weaponisation’ fund
items.summary: The Senate delayed a vote on immigration enforcement funds in a rare show of dissent ahead of its Memorial Day recess.

items.title: Iran war live: ‘Little bit’ of progress in US-Iran talks, Washington says
items.summary: An Israeli strike on Lebanon's Deir Qanoun en-Nahr kills 6, including two rescuers and a Syrian child.

items.title: US says it has not changed its stance on sanctioning Francesca Albanese
items.summary: The Trump administration had sanctioned the UN expert for her criticism of Israel's policies towards Palestinians.

items.title: Over 2,000 gather in San Diego to mourn three men killed in mosque attack
items.summary: The three men killed in the shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego were remembered as heroes at the memorial.

items.title: At least 16 people killed in two attacks in northern Honduras
items.summary: One attack involved a police raid, while the other took place on a palm farm, killing rural labourers.

items.title: Board of Peace envoy warns ‘permanent’ Gaza divide under current status quo
items.summary: UN Security Council urged to press for Israeli obligations and Hamas disarmament under Gaza 'ceasefire' roadmap.

items.title: Ronaldo scores twice to seal Saudi Pro League at last with Al-Nassr
items.summary: Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo leads Al-Nassr to Saudi Pro League title in last game before World Cup 2026.

items.title: Ebola treatment hospital burned down in DRC as outbreak spreads
items.summary: Protesters set a hospital on fire in the Democratic of Congo, as authorities struggle to contain an Ebola outbreak.
